options.label: Long Point 8.5 cm
options.description: The Long Point collar is defined by its dramatic 8.5cm points that angle sharply downward, creating a narrow spread that frames a tie knot with patrician authority. Originating from mid-century American and British tailoring traditions, it conveys formality and a distinctly traditional sensibility that pairs best with a four-in-hand or half-Windsor knot. On the shirt, it reads as elongated and commanding, adding vertical emphasis to the chest and making the wearer appear taller and more formal.

options.label: Fashion Point 5.8 cm
options.description: A short 5.8 cm point cut close and narrow for a sharp, contemporary line. The reduced length keeps the collar neat under a slim tie knot or open at the throat, making it the natural choice for slim-cut, fashion-forward shirts and minimalist Italian styling.

options.label: Regular Collar 22
options.description: The Regular collar is the workhorse of dress shirt tailoring — a moderately spread, versatile collar that works equally well open-necked or with any standard tie knot. At 22mm of collar stand height, it sits comfortably above the jacket lapel without overwhelming the neckline. Its balanced proportions make it the default choice for business and formal occasions, projecting a clean, unfussy sophistication.

options.label: Point 5.8 cm with Button
options.description: A compact 5.8 cm point finished with a throat button that fastens the leaves neatly together. The button anchors the points for a tidy, slightly sporty line whether worn with a tie or open — a versatile, modern take on the classic point.

options.label: Point 7.0 cm
options.description: The classic Point collar at 7.0cm is the most timeless of dress shirt collars — moderate in length, with a slight downward angle that suits a wide range of face shapes and tie knots. It occupies the sweet spot between the long traditional point and the casual button-down, lending a shirt a polished formality without appearing severe. This collar stands well against a jacket lapel and is the standard choice for most bespoke shirtmakers seeking understated elegance.

options.label: Point 7.0 cm with Button
options.description: The timeless 7.0 cm point with an added throat button that secures a cleaner, more controlled roll. The button keeps the points sitting flush to the shirt front, lending a neat, deliberate finish that works equally with or without a tie.

options.label: Point 7.0 cm Hidden Button
options.description: A 7.0 cm point with a button concealed beneath the leaf — the structure of a fastened collar with none of the visible hardware. The hidden closure preserves a smooth, uninterrupted collar line while keeping the points crisply in place.

options.label: Curve Point 7.5 cm
options.description: A 7.5 cm point whose tips are softened into a gentle rounded curve rather than a sharp tip. The rounded termination keeps the length and presence of a full point while reading softer and more approachable, flattering angular faces with or without a tie.

options.label: Point 9.5 cm with Button
options.description: An emphatic 9.5 cm long point fastened with a throat button. The dramatic length adds vertical authority and a distinctly traditional, patrician air, while the button keeps the long leaves disciplined against the chest.

options.label: Regular 8.5 cm with Tab
options.description: A regular collar fitted with a tab — small fabric straps that snap behind the tie knot to lift and arch it forward. The result is a precisely framed, slightly formal knot that sits proud of the collar, a favourite detail of mid-century and English dressing.

options.label: Point 5.8 cm Zigzag Stitching
options.description: A short 5.8 cm point trimmed with decorative zigzag topstitching along the collar edge. The stitch adds a subtle artisanal flourish and visual texture to an otherwise clean point, signalling hand-finished, expressive shirtmaking.

options.label: Round Point 7.0 cm
options.description: A 7.0 cm point with rounded tips — a softened club-style termination that mellows the geometry of a standard point. It lends a quietly vintage, gentle character that pairs especially well with knitted ties and tighter four-in-hand knots.

options.label: Round Point 7.0 cm (Small Holes)
options.description: A 7.0 cm round-tip collar drilled with discreet eyelets to take a collar pin or bar beneath the knot. The pin draws the rounded points together and pushes the tie knot forward, adding Edwardian polish and a deliberate, dressed-up finish.

options.label: Point 7.5 cm (Small Holes)
options.description: A 7.5 cm point prepared with small pinholes for a collar pin or bar. The pin nips the points together under the knot, arching the tie and lending a crisp, formal architecture that elevates an everyday point collar.

options.label: Point 7.5 cm Triangular Patch
options.description: A 7.5 cm point reinforced with a triangular fabric patch at the inner collar — a heritage construction detail that stiffens and stabilises the points. Beyond its function, the patch reads as a mark of traditional, hand-built shirtmaking.

options.label: Hidden Button 7.5 cm
options.description: A clean 7.5 cm point with a concealed button under the leaf for a controlled roll without visible fastenings. It marries the tidiness of a button-down with the sleek front of a dress collar, ideal for a modern, uncluttered look.

options.label: Wrap Collar 7.5 cm
options.description: A 7.5 cm wrap collar whose leaves are cut to overlap and sweep across the throat rather than meet symmetrically. The asymmetric wrap is a distinctive, architectural choice that frames the neck dramatically and stands apart from conventional tailoring.

options.label: Small Square 5.0 cm with Button
options.description: A compact 5.0 cm collar with squared-off tips and a throat button. The blunt square ends give a neat, geometric finish, while the button keeps the short leaves anchored — a tidy, casual-leaning detail for smart everyday shirts.

options.label: Small Square 5.0 cm
options.description: A compact 5.0 cm collar finished with squared rather than pointed tips. The short length and blunt geometry read modern and understated, sitting cleanly under knitwear or worn open for a relaxed, contemporary neckline.

options.label: Small Square 5.0 cm Zigzag
options.description: A small 5.0 cm square collar trimmed with decorative zigzag topstitching. The combination of blunt squared tips and an artisanal stitched edge gives a quietly expressive, hand-finished character to a casual shirt.

options.label: Square 6.5 cm
options.description: A 6.5 cm collar with squared-off points for a clean, architectural termination. The straight ends lend a deliberate, modern geometry that balances well on broader faces and frames a tie knot with crisp precision.

options.label: Square 6.5 cm with Button
options.description: A 6.5 cm squared-tip collar secured with a throat button. The square ends give a structured, contemporary line and the button keeps them flush to the front, suiting a tidy smart-casual look with or without a tie.

options.label: Regular Collar 20
options.description: A balanced, moderately spread everyday collar (pattern #20) sized for proportion and versatility. It sits neatly under a jacket lapel and takes any standard knot, offering clean, unfussy formality as a dependable business default.

options.label: AF-1 7.0 cm
options.description: The AF-1 collar at 7.0 cm offers a balanced semi-spread — wider than a point but short of a full cutaway. This middle ground frames a medium tie knot handsomely while remaining easy to wear open, making it a confident all-rounder.

options.label: Hexagon Collar
options.description: A fashion-forward collar whose leaves are cut into a faceted, six-sided geometric profile. The angular hexagonal shape is a deliberate statement detail that gives an otherwise classic shirt a sculptural, modern edge.

options.label: Middle Cut-Away 7.0 cm
options.description: The Middle Cut-Away at 7.0cm offers a moderate spread where the collar points angle outward at roughly 45 degrees, opening up the collar zone to showcase a larger tie knot. It blends the structure of a point collar with the expansiveness of a true cutaway, making it the ideal everyday formal collar for Italian-influenced dressing. The 7.0cm point length keeps it elegant without tipping into the extreme continental territory.

options.label: Middle Cut-Away 7.0 cm with Button
options.description: A 7.0 cm middle cut-away with the outward-angled spread of a cutaway plus a throat button. The button steadies the wider leaves for a tidy line, marrying continental spread with practical, controlled wear.

options.label: Regular Collar 19
options.description: A moderately spread everyday collar (pattern #19) cut for balanced, versatile proportions. It frames any standard knot cleanly and sits comfortably beneath a jacket — a quiet, reliable choice for business and formal wear.

options.label: Middle Cut-Away 8.5 cm
options.description: The Middle Cut-Away at 8.5cm combines a longer point length with the outward-angled cutaway spread, creating a collar that is both assertive and elegant. The extra length ensures the points sit correctly over the jacket lapels without curling, while the wider spread accommodates full and half-Windsor knots with ease. This collar is favoured by Continental and Neapolitan shirtmakers for its confident balance of tradition and modernity.

options.label: Large Round 5.3 cm
options.description: A larger 5.3 cm club collar with fully rounded tips — the rounder, more pronounced cousin of the small round. Its soft curves evoke Edwardian and Ivy heritage, lending a distinctive, gently nostalgic character best paired with a slim knot or collar pin.

options.label: Round 5.3 cm
options.description: A neat 5.3 cm club collar with rounded tips, compact and softly vintage in feel. Its understated curves give a shirt quiet personality and pair beautifully with a knitted tie or a small four-in-hand.

options.label: Large Cut-Away 8.2 cm
options.description: The Large Cut-Away at 8.2cm is the boldest of the spread collar family, with the points spread nearly horizontally and the collar opening wide enough to frame even a large Windsor knot without crowding. It projects a thoroughly modern, Italian sensibility — worn open-necked, it creates a dramatic V-opening that feels effortlessly smart. On a formal shirt, it commands attention and pairs particularly well with a double-breasted jacket or a prominent tie knot.

options.label: Large Cut-Away 8.0 cm
options.description: An 8.0 cm large cut-away with points spread to a wide, near-horizontal angle — Mediterranean in width and confidence. The expansive opening frames a full Windsor with ease and creates a bold V when worn open, reading thoroughly modern and Italian.

options.label: Cut-Away 5.5 cm with Button
options.description: An entry-level 5.5 cm cut-away with a throat button — a gentle introduction to the spread family. The shorter leaves and modest cutaway angle keep it versatile and easy to wear, while the button maintains a clean, controlled line.

options.label: Cut-Away 5.5 cm
options.description: A versatile 5.5 cm cut-away with a moderate Neapolitan spread and short, easy-wearing points. It opens the neckline just enough to feel relaxed and contemporary, equally at home with a slim tie or worn open at the collar.

options.label: Double Cut-Away 5.5 cm with Button
options.description: A 5.5 cm cut-away with a double-layered leaf construction and throat button, adding subtle depth and structure at the collar edge. The doubling gives a crisper, more substantial roll while the button keeps the spread tidy.

options.label: Large Cut-Away 7.5 cm
options.description: The Large Cut-Away at 7.5cm delivers the full dramatic spread of a wide cutaway collar with a slightly shorter point length that suits men with narrower faces or smaller collar bones. The wide horizontal angle of the points creates a spacious opening that is excellent for wearing without a tie in business casual contexts. This collar reads as sophisticated and European, carrying the signature of Neapolitan and Milanese tailoring houses.

options.label: Curved 8.0 cm
options.description: The Curved collar at 8.0cm features points that follow a gentle concave arc from the collar band outward, rather than projecting in a straight line. This subtle curvature gives the collar a softer, more naturalistic silhouette that moves with the body and tends to lie flat without collar stays. It is a hallmark of traditional British and some Neapolitan shirtmaking, conveying a relaxed formality that ages exceptionally well.

options.label: Curved 9.0 cm
options.description: A wider 9.0 cm collar whose long points follow a gentle concave arc rather than a straight line. The extra length and soft curvature create a relaxed, flowing silhouette that tends to lie flat without stays — quietly elegant and traditional.

options.label: French 7.5 cm
options.description: The French collar at 7.5cm is the continental cutaway par excellence — extremely wide-spread, with points that angle dramatically toward the shoulder seams to accommodate large tie knots or look spectacular when worn open. Born from French and Neapolitan tailoring, it is the collar most associated with Roman Holiday sprezzatura and the Dolce Vita aesthetic. When worn open, the two points frame the cravat zone boldly, making it a statement collar for confident dressers.

options.label: Regular Collar 21
options.description: A moderately spread everyday collar (pattern #21) proportioned for clean versatility. It frames any standard knot neatly and sits well under a lapel, an unobtrusive, dependable choice for business dressing.

options.label: French 8.0 cm
options.description: A wide 8.0 cm French spread with points angled boldly toward the shoulders. Continental and assured, it accommodates a large Windsor knot and frames the throat dramatically when worn open — a statement collar for confident dressers.

options.label: Stand Collar 5.0 cm
options.description: A 5.0 cm upright band collar with no fold-over leaf, circling the neck cleanly in the Mandarin or Nehru manner. Minimalist and architectural, it offers a modern, tie-free formality that reads distinctive against conventional Western tailoring.

options.label: One Collar (Band / Mandarin)
options.description: The Mandarin or Band-Only collar is simply the collar stand without the attached fold-over collar leaf — a short upright band of fabric that circles the neck cleanly and closes with a single button. It creates a minimalist, architectural look inspired by East Asian dress that reads as modern and iconoclastic in the context of Western tailoring. Worn open, it frames the neck with elegant restraint; worn closed, it provides a clean, tie-free formality.

options.label: Tuxedo Collar
options.description: The Wing collar is the most ceremonially formal shirt collar in existence, featuring two small triangular points that fold outward like a pair of wings and are worn exclusively with a bow tie for black-tie or white-tie events. The stiff, outward-projecting wings frame the bow tie with architectural precision, completing the strict formal dress code where no other collar would suffice. On a pleated-front dress shirt with double cuffs, it represents the apex of Western formal dress.

options.label: Rounded Stand Collar
options.description: The Rounded Stand collar — often called a Club collar — features a short collar stand with rounded rather than pointed tips, evoking the Edwardian era and the dress codes of elite English boarding schools. The absence of pointed ends creates a soft, almost innocent quality that is at once vintage and modern, lending shirts a quietly distinctive character that stands apart from conventional tailoring. It pairs exceptionally well with a small tied cravat or a knitted tie worn with a tight four-in-hand.

options.label: Squared Stand Collar
options.description: The Squared Stand collar is a rare, early-twentieth-century form in which the collar points are cut with a straight, squared-off end rather than tapering to a tip or rounding to a curve. This blunt geometric termination gives the collar an almost architectural quality, referencing the detachable collars of Edwardian formal dress while feeling deliberately modern in a bespoke context. It is a confident choice for those who want their shirt to make a quiet but unmistakable statement about historical sartorial knowledge.

options.label: Hexagon Stand Collar
options.description: A band-style stand collar finished with a faceted, six-sided geometric profile at the closure. The hexagonal detailing turns a simple upright band into a sculptural, fashion-forward focal point.

options.label: Square Stand (No Button)
options.description: An upright band collar with squared ends and no buttoned closure, leaving a clean open break at the throat. The blunt geometry and absence of a fastening give a relaxed, modern minimalism suited to soft, casual fabrics.

options.label: Rolled Stand Collar
options.description: A banded stand collar with a soft rolled top edge rather than a stiff upright finish. The gentle roll relaxes the neckline for casual elegance, bridging the structure of a band collar with an easy, unstudied drape.

options.label: One-Piece 7.5 cm with Tab
options.description: A 7.5 cm one-piece collar — leaf and stand cut as a single panel with no band seam — fitted with a tab. The seamless construction gives a soft, natural Neapolitan roll, while the tab lifts and frames the tie knot.

options.label: One-Piece 8.0 cm with Tab
options.description: An 8.0 cm one-piece collar cut without a separate stand seam for a fluid, soft roll, completed with a tab behind the knot. The seamless leaf drapes gently around the neck, a hallmark of relaxed Neapolitan shirtmaking.

options.label: One-Piece 8.5 cm with Tab
options.description: An 8.5 cm one-piece collar with leaf and stand in a single panel and a tab closure. The longer seamless leaf rolls softly and sits handsomely over a jacket, lifting the tie knot for a refined, lived-in elegance.

options.label: One-Piece 9.0 cm with Tab
options.description: A generous 9.0 cm one-piece collar — the tallest of the seamless range — with a tab to arch the knot. Its long, soft roll makes a confident, distinctly Neapolitan statement while remaining graceful and unstructured.

options.label: Standard with 7.5 cm Collar Stand
options.description: A standard collar mounted on a tall 7.5 cm stand, raising the collar higher around the neck. The elevated band lends a more formal, elongating posture and keeps the collar standing proud above a jacket lapel.

options.label: Point 8.5 cm with Button
options.description: A long 8.5 cm point fastened with a throat button. The extended leaves add traditional, elongating authority while the button keeps them disciplined and flush against the chest for a controlled, formal line.

options.label: Cuban Collar
options.description: The Cuban collar — also known as a camp collar or revere collar — features a wide, lapel-like fold that lies flat against the shirt front with no band, no points, and no buttoning at the throat. It creates an open, relaxed neckline that evokes mid-century resort wear and Caribbean dressing, carrying an air of louche elegance. On bespoke shirts it is typically cut in luxury fabrics like silk or linen, making it ideal for warm-weather evenings and smart casual occasions.

options.label: DR Point 5.8 cm
options.description: A narrow 5.8 cm point in the DR pattern — a slim, modern point cut for a sharp, close line. Its compact proportions suit slim shirts and small knots, reading clean and contemporary whether dressed or worn open.

options.label: Button-Down 7.8 cm Stand
options.description: The Button-Down collar at 7.8cm stand is the quintessential American shirt collar, featuring small buttons at each point that fasten to the shirt front to maintain the signature soft roll over a tie knot. This rolling of the collar creates a casual, lived-in elegance that is the calling card of Oxford Cloth Button-Down shirts and Ivy League dressing. Without a tie it produces a natural, unfussy frame for the neck that works from weekend brunches to smart casual office environments.

options.label: Frog-Fastening Mandarin (Rounded)
options.description: A rounded Mandarin band closed with a decorative frog knot rather than a button. The looped frog fastening lends an ornamental, heritage Asian character, turning a minimalist stand collar into an expressive focal detail.

answers.label: Spread & Cutaway
answers.description: Wider spread — modern, confident, great with a tie.

answers.label: Casual & Band
answers.description: Button-down, Cuban, mandarin — relaxed and expressive.

options.label: Machine 0.1 cm Top Stitching
options.description: Very fine machine topstitch 0.1 cm from edge.

options.label: Machine 0.3 cm Top Stitching
options.description: Fine machine topstitch 0.3 cm from edge.

options.label: Machine 0.5 cm Top Stitching
options.description: Medium machine topstitch 0.5 cm from edge.

options.label: Lower Part of Top Collar
options.description: Contrast on lower area of top collar.

options.label: Upper Part of Top Collar
options.description: Contrast on upper area of top collar.

options.label: Strip in Middle Top Collar
options.description: Contrast strip through center of top collar.

options.label: Collar Point Opening Edge
options.description: Contrast on collar point opening edge.

options.label: 4 cm to Collar Point Opening Edge
options.description: Contrast splicing 4 cm from collar point.

options.label: 1.5 cm to Collar Point Opening Edge
options.description: Contrast splicing 1.5 cm from collar point.

options.label: Single Left Placket Edge
options.description: Contrast on single left placket edge.

options.label: Double Left Placket Edges
options.description: Contrast on both left placket edges.

options.label: Top Collar Edge
options.description: Contrast trim on top collar edge.

options.label: Upper Sleeve Cuff
options.description: Contrast on upper portion of sleeve cuff.

options.label: Lower Sleeve Cuff
options.description: Contrast on lower portion of sleeve cuff.

options.label: Strip in Middle Cuff
options.description: Contrast strip through center of cuff.

options.label: None
options.description: A shirt made without collar stays relies entirely on the quality of its interlining and construction to maintain the shape and stiffness of the collar points. This is standard on soft, unstructured collars — particularly Neapolitan-style shirts — where the collar is designed to drape naturally rather than project rigidly. The result is a lived-in, un-fussed collar posture that is intrinsic to sprezzatura dressing.

options.label: Removable
options.description: Removable collar stays are slim rigid strips — in metal, plastic, or mother-of-pearl — inserted into small pockets sewn into the underside of each collar point to keep the points flat and from curling upward through the day. They can be removed before laundering and reinserted afterward, giving the wearer control over how structured or relaxed the collar appears. Metal collar stays in brass or stainless steel are the choice of the careful dresser who treats his shirts as precision instruments.

options.label: Fixed (Permanent)
options.description: Fixed or permanent collar stays are thin strips of rigid material — typically plastic — that are sewn directly into the collar interlining during shirt construction, creating a collar that maintains its shape permanently without any action required from the wearer. This is the most convenient collar stay system, ensuring the collar always looks pristine, though the stays cannot be removed if they soften over time with repeated laundering. They are common on ready-to-wear dress shirts and some made-to-measure constructions.

options.label: Hidden Button (No Stay)
options.description: The hidden button collar stay system uses small buttons sewn to the underside of the collar point that fasten through a buttonhole on the shirt yoke or front — functionally similar to a button-down collar but invisible from the outside. This hybrid system maintains the clean, uncluttered look of a spread or cutaway collar while ensuring the points lie flat and still without visible buttons. It is a signature feature of Eton shirts and some Continental bespoke makers who value structured collar presentation with formal aesthetics.

options.label: Round Collar Stand
options.description: Curved round stand — soft comfort fit.

fields.label: Buttonhole Thread Color on Collar Band

options.label: Plain Placket
options.description: The Plain placket — also called the standard or French front placket — features a strip of doubled fabric stitched onto the shirt front with visible parallel lines of stitching flanking each side. It is the most common shirt front construction and offers a clean, structured appearance that is equally appropriate for business and casual wear. The visible topstitching gives it a confident, workmanlike quality that ages gracefully.

options.label: One Piece with Cuban Collar (Tab)
options.description: One-piece construction with Cuban collar tab.

options.label: Outer Placket
options.description: The Outer placket features the button strip sitting visibly on the exterior of the shirt front as a separate applied piece of fabric, creating a clearly defined band that contrasts slightly with the body of the shirt. This construction is common on casual and work-wear shirts, lending a utilitarian robustness and a relaxed, non-fussy aesthetic. On a bespoke shirt, it reads as deliberately unformal — a conscious choice toward casual refinement over boardroom precision.

options.label: Hidden Placket
options.description: The Hidden placket — also called the Covered or Fly-front placket — conceals the buttons behind an extra flap of shirt fabric that folds over the button line, so the front of the shirt presents an unbroken, smooth expanse of cloth. This is the most formal and elegant placket choice, favoured for dress shirts and tuxedo shirts where a clean, uninterrupted line from collar to waist is desirable. It imparts a refined, almost sculptural look to the shirt front.

options.label: Stud Hidden Placket (2 Buttons)
options.description: The Stud Hidden placket is a hidden/covered placket designed specifically to accommodate formal shirt studs in place of buttons — the placket conceals the stud holes beneath its flap for a perfectly smooth, uninterrupted white shirtfront. A variation found on bespoke dinner shirts and some dress shirts, it combines the clean line of the hidden placket with the mechanical drama of gold or mother-of-pearl studs peeking through small openings. This is the standard construction for white tie and black tie formal shirts.

options.label: Hidden Turn-Up Placket with Knots
options.description: The Hidden Turnup Placket with Knots is an elaborate bespoke construction in which the hidden placket is finished with hand-worked fabric knots at each button position, visible when the flap is opened. These twisted or knotted button loops are a signature detail of Neapolitan handmade shirtmaking — they replace standard buttons with soft fabric toggles and represent the highest tier of shirt craftsmanship. The effect is discreet from outside but reveals extraordinary hand-finishing when inspected closely.

options.label: Half Hidden Placket
options.description: The Half Hidden placket is a hybrid construction: the upper portion of the button strip is concealed behind a fold while the lower section is left visible as a conventional plain placket. This creates a subtle transition in formality down the shirt front — more formal and clean at the chest where it is most visible under a jacket, more casual toward the waist. It is a particularly versatile choice for shirts that will be worn both tucked with a jacket and untucked on casual occasions.

options.label: 0.1 cm Topstitch (Inner Plain Placket)
options.description: Fine stitch on inner hidden placket edge.

options.label: Horizontal (2nd from Bottom)
options.description: Second buttonhole from bottom is horizontal.

options.label: Lower Part of Top Collar
options.description: Contrast on lower area of top collar.

options.label: Upper Part of Top Collar
options.description: Contrast on upper area of top collar.

options.label: Strip in Middle Top Collar
options.description: Contrast strip through center of top collar.

options.label: Collar Point Opening Edge
options.description: Contrast on collar point opening edge.

options.label: 4 cm to Collar Point Opening Edge
options.description: Contrast splicing 4 cm from collar point.

options.label: 1.5 cm to Collar Point Opening Edge
options.description: Contrast splicing 1.5 cm from collar point.

options.label: Single Left Placket Edge
options.description: Contrast on single left placket edge.

options.label: Double Left Placket Edges
options.description: Contrast on both left placket edges.

options.label: Top Collar Edge
options.description: Contrast trim on top collar edge.

options.label: Upper Sleeve Cuff
options.description: Contrast on upper portion of sleeve cuff.

options.label: Lower Sleeve Cuff
options.description: Contrast on lower portion of sleeve cuff.

options.label: Strip in Middle Cuff
options.description: Contrast strip through center of cuff.

options.label: 7 cm
options.description: First button 7 cm from neckline — standard.

options.label: No Pocket
options.description: Choosing no chest pocket leaves the shirt front completely clean and uninterrupted. The absence of a pocket is considered the dressiest and most formal option, keeping the front smooth under a tie or jacket. It gives the shirt a minimal, elegant appearance and is standard on tuxedo and most fitted dress shirts.

options.label: Round Pocket
options.description: A round chest pocket is a patch pocket with curved, rounded bottom corners sewn to the left breast. The softened corners make it the most common and versatile pocket shape, reading as classic and unobtrusive. It adds a touch of casual practicality without disrupting the shirt's overall clean lines.

options.label: Hexagon Pocket
options.description: A hexagon chest pocket has angled lower corners that come to a shallow point, giving the patch a six-sided, faceted outline. The geometric shape is more distinctive and slightly more formal-looking than a plain rounded pocket. It adds a subtle bit of sartorial character to the breast of the shirt.

options.label: Triangle Pocket
options.description: A triangle chest pocket tapers to a single point at the bottom, forming a pointed V-shaped base rather than a flat or rounded edge. This sharp, angular shape is a bolder, more eye-catching detail that draws attention to the breast. It gives the shirt a more vintage or stylized character compared with the standard rounded pocket.

options.label: Round Pocket with Welt
options.description: A round pocket with a welt is a rounded patch pocket finished with a reinforced welt or pleat detail at the opening, adding structure and a more tailored look. The welt gives the pocket mouth a clean, finished edge and a touch of depth. It combines the soft rounded shape with a more sartorial, constructed appearance.

options.label: Round Hem
options.description: The Round hem — also called the shirttail hem — curves upward at the sides from a longer front and back tail, creating the classic dress shirt profile that stays tucked securely into trousers. The rising curve at each side seam reduces bulk at the hip when tucked while ensuring adequate length at front and back to remain anchored throughout the day. This is the standard hem shape for formal and business dress shirts worldwide.

options.label: Straight Hem
options.description: The Straight hem runs at an even, level height all the way around the shirt bottom, with a small vent at each side seam for freedom of movement. It is the preferred hem for shirts designed to be worn untucked — the clean horizontal line creates a polished, deliberate silhouette that avoids the rumpled droop of a round hem worn loose. On casual and resort shirts, a straight hem signals that the garment was designed from the outset to be worn outside the trousers.

options.label: Squared Bottom with Side Vents 5 cm
options.description: Square hem with 5 cm side vents.

options.label: Round Hem with Pentagon Gusset
options.description: Rounded hem with pentagon side gusset.

options.label: Round Hem with Hexagon Gusset
options.description: Rounded hem with hexagon side gusset.

options.label: Large Round Hem with Triangle Gusset
options.description: Larger rounded hem with triangle gusset.

options.label: Small Round Hem with Pentagon Gusset
options.description: Shorter rounded hem with pentagon gusset.

options.label: Customer Button Cuff
options.description: The customer button cuff is the standard barrel cuff: a single squared band of fabric that wraps the wrist and fastens with one or two buttons. It is the most versatile and everyday-appropriate cuff, giving the shirt a clean, business-ready finish that works equally under a jacket or worn on its own. Because it requires no cufflinks, it reads as practical and understated rather than formal.

options.label: French Cuff — 2 Buttons, 1 Buttonhole
options.description: The French cuff, or double cuff, is an extra-long cuff that folds back on itself so the doubled fabric is fastened through aligned buttonholes with cufflinks rather than buttons. The folded layer creates a substantial, structured cuff that sits crisply below the jacket sleeve and is the most formal everyday option, ideal for evening wear and dressy business settings. The visible cufflink turns the wrist into a point of jewelry-like detail, elevating the whole shirt.

options.label: French Cuff — 6 Buttonholes
options.description: This is a French cuff configured with six buttonholes, meaning each folded leaf carries extra holes so the cuff can be linked at multiple points or fitted to different wrist depths. The additional buttonholes allow the cuff to be cinched more precisely and give the closure a more engineered, bespoke appearance. Visually it remains a classic squared double cuff, but the extra perforations signal a more customized, tailored construction.

options.label: French Cuff — 4 Buttonholes
options.description: A French cuff with four buttonholes is the conventional double-cuff arrangement, with two aligned holes on each leaf so a single cufflink passes cleanly through all four layers when folded. This is the standard cufflink closure and produces the familiar crisp, squared double cuff that frames the cufflink neatly. It offers a slightly cleaner, more traditional look than higher buttonhole counts while retaining full formality.

options.label: Curved Cuff — 1 Button, 1 Buttonhole
options.description: The curved one-button cuff is a barrel cuff whose outer corners are rounded off rather than left square, fastened with a single button and one buttonhole. The softened corners give the wrist a gentler, more refined silhouette than a sharp squared cuff while keeping the simplicity of a single-button closure. It is a subtle, slightly dressier take on the everyday barrel cuff.

options.label: Hexagon French Cuff — 4 Buttonholes
options.description: The hexagon French cuff is a double cuff whose folded edge is cut with an angled, mitered corner so the silhouette reads as a faceted hexagonal shape rather than a plain square. Worn with cufflinks like any French cuff, the angled cut adds a distinctive, sartorial geometry that catches the eye at the wrist. It keeps full formal status while giving the cuff a more bespoke, characterful outline.

options.label: Round Cuff — 2 Buttons, 1 Buttonhole
options.description: The round two-button cuff is a barrel cuff with rounded outer corners secured by two buttons stacked along its depth. The pair of buttons makes the cuff taller and dressier than a one-button version and provides extra room to accommodate a watch, while the rounded corners soften the look. It strikes a balance between formal polish and everyday wearability.

options.label: Curved French Style — 2 Buttonholes
options.description: The curved French cuff is a double cuff with its folded-back outer corner rounded off rather than squared, fastened with cufflinks through two buttonholes. The rounded edge gives a softer, slightly more relaxed take on the classic formal cuff without reducing its dressiness. It is favored by those who want the elegance of a French cuff with a gentler, less rigid line.

options.label: Round Cuff — 2 Buttons, 2 Buttonholes
options.description: Rounded barrel cuff, 2 buttons and 2 buttonholes.

options.label: French Cuff — 4 Buttonholes (Variant)
options.description: French cuff with 4 buttonholes, variant cut.

options.label: Rounded Cuff with Pleats — 1 Button, 1 Bh
options.description: Rounded barrel with pleats at cuff.

options.label: Round Cuff with Pleats — 1 Button, 1 Bh
options.description: Rounded barrel with pleats at cuff.

options.label: Square Cuff — 3 Buttons, 3 Buttonholes
options.description: Square cuff with triple button and buttonhole.

options.label: Square Cuff — 2 Buttons, 2 Buttonholes
options.description: Square cuff with 2 buttons and 2 buttonholes.

options.label: Round French Cuff — 4 Buttonholes
options.description: Rounded French double cuff with 4 buttonholes.

answers.label: French / Double Cuff
answers.description: Folded back, worn with cufflinks — formal and refined.

options.label: 5.0 cm
options.description: A 5.0cm cuff width is a relatively narrow, compact cuff that sits close to the wrist for a trim, modern proportion. The shorter depth keeps the cuff light and unobtrusive, pairing well with slimmer sleeves and a streamlined silhouette. It is the most understated of the width options and reveals less cuff below a jacket sleeve.

options.label: 6.0 cm
options.description: A 6.0cm cuff width is a mid-range, classic depth that suits most builds and is a safe default for a business shirt. It offers enough fabric to hold a crisp shape and frame a watch without looking oversized. This balanced proportion shows a modest, tidy band of cuff beyond the jacket sleeve.

options.label: 6.5 cm
options.description: A 6.5cm cuff width adds a touch more depth than the standard, giving a slightly more substantial, formal presence at the wrist. The added height makes it especially comfortable over a watch and lends the cuff a more deliberate, tailored proportion. It reads as a refined upgrade from the everyday default width.

options.label: 7.0 cm
options.description: A 7.0cm cuff width is a tall, generous cuff that makes a confident, traditional statement at the wrist. The extra depth holds structure well and is well suited to two-button closures and dressier shirts. It shows a prominent band of cuff below the jacket sleeve, reading as classic and assertive.

options.label: 8.0 cm
options.description: An 8.0cm cuff width is the deepest option, producing a bold, dramatic cuff that dominates the wrist with a stately, old-school formality. The substantial height is most at home on French cuffs and evening shirts where presence is desired. It projects the most fabric beyond a jacket sleeve and signals a deliberately grand, sartorial choice.

options.label: Normal
options.description: Normal cuff lining uses a standard-weight fused interlining that gives the cuff a balanced amount of structure, holding a crisp shape without feeling stiff. It is the default choice for most dress shirts, keeping the cuff neat and presentable through the day. The result is a clean, professional cuff that is neither floppy nor rigid.

options.label: Soft
options.description: Soft cuff lining uses a lighter, unfused or lightly brushed interlining so the cuff drapes with a relaxed, supple feel and a little natural pucker. It suits casual and unstructured shirts, giving the wrist a softer, more lived-in appearance rather than a sharp formal edge. The cuff moves more freely and feels gentler against the skin.

options.label: Hard
options.description: Hard cuff lining uses a heavier, firmly fused interlining that keeps the cuff stiff, flat, and razor-sharp throughout wear. It is the most formal option, ideal for evening and French cuffs where a rigid, structured edge enhances the dressy effect. The cuff resists wrinkling and maintains a clean, architectural line at the wrist.

options.label: Single Pleat
options.description: A single pleat at the cuff is one small fold of fabric set where the sleeve gathers into the cuff, easing the fuller sleeve into the narrower cuff opening. This single tuck creates a tidy, minimal gather and a clean transition at the wrist. It is the more understated of the cuff-pleat options, keeping the sleeve neat without excess bulk.

options.label: Double Pleat
options.description: A double pleat at the cuff places two small folds of fabric where the sleeve meets the cuff, distributing the gather across two tucks. This handles a fuller sleeve more smoothly and is regarded as a more traditional, refined shirtmaking detail. The paired pleats give the wrist a slightly more structured, sartorial finish than a single pleat.

options.label: Upper Sleeve Cuff
options.description: Contrast on upper portion of sleeve cuff.

options.label: Lower Sleeve Cuff
options.description: Contrast on lower portion of sleeve cuff.

options.label: Strip in Middle Cuff
options.description: Contrast strip through center of cuff.

options.label: Double Darts
options.description: Double darts in the back are two vertical seams stitched on either side of the spine that remove excess fabric through the midsection. They taper the shirt sharply from the shoulders to the waist, creating a slim, fitted silhouette that follows the body. This is the dressiest, most modern back treatment and is popular for a clean, tailored look.

options.label: Center Pleats
options.description: A center pleat is a single fold of fabric placed at the middle of the back just under the yoke, adding a few inches of extra movement across the shoulders. It is a classic, slightly more casual detail commonly seen on traditional dress shirts, giving room to reach forward comfortably. Visually it creates a neat vertical line down the center back.

options.label: Side Pleat
options.description: Side pleats, also called rear or knife pleats, are two folds positioned below the yoke near each shoulder blade rather than at the center. Each adds a small amount of fabric for ease of movement while keeping the center back clean and uninterrupted. They are a discreet, professional way to add room without the casual look of a center pleat.

options.label: Box Pleat to Bottom with Double Darts
options.description: Full box pleat plus two darts.

options.label: Inverted Center Pleats
options.description: Inverted center pleats are folds turned inward toward the centerline so the pleat's depth is hidden beneath a smooth surface at the center back. This yields a clean exterior appearance while still releasing extra fabric for movement underneath. The effect is a tidy, refined back that combines comfort with a more polished, concealed pleat.

options.label: Napoli Yoke with Double Darts
options.description: The Napoli yoke with double darts pairs a Neapolitan-style yoke construction with two shaping darts at the back, blending soft tailoring with a fitted waist. The darts draw the shirt in through the midsection for a trim line while the yoke gives a clean shoulder. It is a sartorial, southern-Italian-inspired finish favored for a refined, body-following fit.

options.label: Box Pleat to Bottom
options.description: A box pleat to the bottom is a wider central pleat formed by two folds meeting to make a flat squared panel that runs down the back below the yoke. It supplies the most generous amount of extra fabric of the pleat options, maximizing freedom of movement. The look is relaxed and traditional, well suited to fuller-cut and casual shirts.

options.label: Side Pleats with Double Darts
options.description: Side pleats with double darts combine rear knife pleats near the shoulder blades with two shaping darts lower down the back. The pleats add ease across the upper back while the darts nip in the waist, giving both comfort up top and a tapered fit below. This hybrid back offers movement and a tailored silhouette at once.

options.label: Napoli without Dart
options.description: Neapolitan clean back — no dart, no pleat.

options.label: Normal Yoke
options.description: A normal yoke is a single continuous panel of fabric spanning shoulder to shoulder across the top of the back. It is the standard, economical construction with the grain running in one direction and no center seam. The result is a clean, uninterrupted upper back with no pattern break, which is perfectly suited to solid fabrics.

options.label: Half Yoke
options.description: A half or split yoke is divided by a center seam into two pieces, allowing each half to be cut on an angle and the pattern mirrored or matched at the join. It is considered the more refined, bespoke construction because it enables stripe and check matching across the back and finer fit adjustment at the shoulders. On patterned cloth it creates a striking chevron or mirrored effect down the center back.

options.label: None (Standard Grain)
options.description: Fabric cut on straight grain — standard.

options.label: Outer Top Collar
options.description: Bias cut applied to outer top collar.

options.label: Inner Collar Stand
options.description: Bias cut on inner collar stand.

options.label: Outer Collar Stand
options.description: Bias cut on outer collar stand.

options.label: Inner Top Collar
options.description: Bias cut on inner top collar.

options.label: Outer Cuff
options.description: Bias cut applied to outer cuff.

options.label: Pocket Cuff
options.description: Bias cut on pocket and cuff.

options.label: Lower Wrap Collar
options.description: Contrast on lower wrap collar area.

options.label: Upper Wrap Collar
options.description: Contrast on upper wrap collar area.

options.label: Inner Yoke
options.description: Contrast fabric on inner yoke panel.
